what_it_does: A grounding/confirmation result for the A3-inspector-desktop.html mockup (the Node Inspector config panel for a process node). Holds 46 candidate dossiers reduced to 27 clusters, each with a ui:// address, capabilities, grounding flag, the what/what-you-can-do/how-to-change triple, a maps_to_feature id, and a 3-draw jury verdict sorting them into confirmed (7) and flagged (20).
observations: ground block describes the Node Inspector as a panel that opens on clicking a process node, exposing model/temperature/max-tokens and a force re-run.
Counts: n_candidates 46, n_dossiers 46, n_clusters 27 — the largest candidate set in this batch.
Confirmed: force trigger (NODE-force), process-11 label, node memo card, llm-process node, model-field, inspector/act/force (ENG-force, unanimous), inspector inbox (INB-lanes).
How-to text cites Inspector.tsx, NodeConfigForm.tsx, contracts/node_type.py, runtime/scheduler.py, and suite.py:available_models as the backing code regions.
Many config-field dossiers (top_p, max_tokens, base_url, endpoint, retries) are 'built' yet flagged with 0–1 of 3 jury grounding.
A large 7-member cluster (ui://inspector/ai-model) collapses many AI-model-config detections into one flagged dossier.
ENG-force how-to explicitly references doRun([nodeId]) and force-past-memo behaviour in runtime/scheduler.py.
standouts: Concrete code-path citations (doRun([nodeId]) → scheduler, available_models registry, config_schema-driven fields) tie UI fields to real runtime functions.
The model-enum dossier notes a fallback behavior: a saved model missing from the live list is displayed rather than dropped.
Force-re-run / bypass-memo is the dominant confirmed capability, appearing across several addresses (inspector/force, inspector/act/force).
conventions: Same audit schema as the rest of the batch (ground / counts / confirmed / flagged, howto triple, verdict object).
ui://inspector/<field> addressing aligned to config-form fields.
concerns: High flag rate (20 of 27 clusters) — many 'built' config fields fail jury grounding, suggesting the descriptions may not be verifiable against the rendered element.
NODE-status and NODE-act fail refcheck as non-inventory ids despite plausible config semantics.
Multiple near-duplicate inspector addresses (force, act/force, run-trigger, ai-model, llm-config) describe overlapping re-run/config intents.
notes: The desktop Node-Inspector slice of the UI-grounding audit; rich in config-field elements with explicit code-path citations, but with the batch's highest flag rate and several fabricated feature ids.
questions: Why do so many 'built' config fields receive 0–1 jury grounding — is the jury seeing the field in the rendered mockup at all?
Should NODE-status / NODE-act be added to the inventory, given the config panel clearly exposes status and act semantics?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: an honest grounding audit of the Node Inspector mockup's config controls.
apparent_themes: Node configuration and per-node sampling parameters
Force re-run / bypass-memo as a first-class action
Registry-driven model selection with safe fallback
intention_signals: Aims to ground the Node Inspector's config surface honestly — confirming code-backed controls (force, model field, inbox) while flagging the many config fields and fabricated feature ids that the jury could not verify.
novelty: Explicit runtime function citations (doRun, available_models, config_schema) and the missing-model fallback behavior captured in a UI dossier.
contribution: Contributes the inspector-desktop grounding-audit record: the per-field config inventory, the force/memo and registry-fallback semantics, and the confirmed/flagged verdicts a fused system would keep as the node-inspector grounding evidence.
summary_for_embedding: A UI-grounding audit result for the A3 desktop Node Inspector mockup, clustering 46 config-field dossiers into 7 confirmed and 20 flagged via refcheck and a 3-draw soft jury.
intention_for_embedding: To verify each Node Inspector config control is truthfully grounded in real code and flag unverifiable fields and fabricated feature ids for operator review.
